# UC-19.1.38 ┬Ę Proxmox QEMU/KVM Live Migration Task Failure Detection

## Description

Consumes PVE `cluster/tasks` history rows in `sourcetype=proxmox:task:qmigrate` (scripted API poll) where `type` is `qmigrate`, normalises source and guest keys with `coalesce(source_node,node,host)` and `coalesce(vmid,id)`, lowercases the PVE `status` string to `status_norm`, filters out success (`ok`) and in-flight (`running`) rows, maps remaining text to `reason_norm` with `match()`-driven buckets (`cpu_flag_mismatch`, `local_storage_unreachable`, `network_disconnect`, `excessive_dirty_pages`, `target_resource_exhausted`, `firewall_blocked`, `qmp_command_failed`, `iso_attached`, `pci_passthrough_present`, `corosync_unhealthy`, `timeout`, else `other_error`), estimates `duration_sec` from `endtime`ŌłÆ`starttime`, aggregates a rolling 24h view per `source_node`+`vmid` with `stats` including `failures_24h`, `reasons`, and `initiated_by`, and assigns a `severity_bucket` where configuration-class failures and resource or membership pressure outrank a single blip. Optional `proxmox:pveproxy:access` provides who hit the migrate API when joined by `vmid` and time.

## Value

Rolling reboots, rack moves, and firmware windows depend on guests actually landing on a healthy node while operators still have room to re-run. A failed online migration that stays invisible until a second fault risks leadership believing the estate is already drained when half the guests are still on the old host, and it complicates RTO and change evidence when a maintenance ticket is already closed. Surfacing `qmigrate` failures with machine-class reasons, a durable `last_upid`, and repeat counts in 24h gives the platform and application teams a time-bounded path to retune CPU models, free RAM on the target, open migration firewall ranges, or remove ISO and passthrough blockers before a narrow window expires. The outcome is fewer stuck evacuations, cleaner hand-offs between SRE and storage or cluster admins, and an audit trail that ties each failed move to a concrete PVE task row and operator drilldown instead of a vague ŌĆ£the VM is slowŌĆØ report.

## Implementation

Deploy `proxmox-monitoring` on a forwarder with HTTPS to the PVE API, place API token material in a restricted file, enable the `poll_qmigrate_tasks` scripted input to `sourcetype=proxmox:task:qmigrate`, add `monitor` for `pveproxy` access, register `proxmox_vm_inventory.csv` if you need owner routing, save the `spl` as a scheduled search with 5ŌĆō15 minute cadence, throttle on `source_node`+`vmid`, and pack drilldowns with `https://<node>:8006/?#v1:0:tasks` plus raw-search tokens for the same `earliest` as `last_failure`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
ŌĆó Proxmox VE 7.x or 8.x cluster with QEMU/KVM guests where live migration (online `qmigrate`) is part of runbooks, maintenance, or host drain.
ŌĆó A Splunk Universal or Heavy Forwarder with HTTPS to the PVE API (port 8006) or, if you only file-monitor, read access to `/var/log/pveproxy/access.log` and task paths on each node. Python 3 for the API poller.
ŌĆó Search-time access to `index=virtualization` (or your standard) and a role that can schedule alerts over 24h windows.
ŌĆó API token for a least-privilege user: `VM.Migrate` (or equivalent for migration), `Sys.Audit` (cluster task visibility), and `Datastore.Audit` if you enrich with storage context. The poller should emit one minified JSON line per task row from `GET /api2/json/cluster/tasks` with `typefilter=qmigrate` and `source=archive` where your build supports historical rows.
ŌĆó Optional: a `lookups/proxmox_vm_inventory.csv` keyed on `vmid` for `owner_team`, `sla_class`, and `pci_passthrough_devices` so you can post-filter alerts (not in the base SPL) or enrich dashboards.


## Step 1 Onboard Proxmox Migration Telemetry

You need a primary structured feed and a useful secondary for attribution.

Path A (recommended): REST API task polling. The cluster task list returns per-row `upid`, `type` (here `qmigrate`), `status` (`OK`, `running`, or an error string), `node` (source), `id` (vmid as string), `user`, and epoch `starttime` / `endtime` when the task is terminal. The poller should call a URL shaped like `https://<cluster-or-node>:8006/api2/json/cluster/tasks?typefilter=qmigrate&limit=500` and optionally `source=archive` so completed failures are not dropped after rotation. If your security model forbids cluster-wide calls, run the same `typefilter` per node: `/api2/json/nodes/<name>/tasks`. Emit each `data[]` object as a single line to stdout with `sourcetype=proxmox:task:qmigrate` and set `_time` from `starttime` when present.

The Python poller (pattern matches UC-19.1.40 style) should map API keys faithfully: if the JSON includes a `target` or target node in nested form, flatten it to a top-level `target_node` field in the printed line so the SPL `values(target_node)` step is populated; if the API only exposes destination in a detail fetch, the minimum viable poller can leave `target_node` null and still classify failures from `status` text.

Path B: `monitor` on `/var/log/pveproxy/access.log` with the stanza below. This captures who called `POST` against `/nodes/<src>/qemu/<vmid>/migrate` and query parameters, but it does not replace Path A for machine-readable `status` and duration. Use B to enrich operator narratives (ŌĆ£initiated from UI session XŌĆØ) or to detect migration attempts that never created a resolvable task row in your poll window.

Path C (optional): forward per-task on-disk files under `/var/log/pve/tasks/*/<UPID>` if your packaging can tag a stable sourcetype; multiline text then needs separate props (see Step 2). Most teams start with A plus B.

`inputs.conf` (adjust `$SPLUNK_HOME` and paths; Heavy Forwarder is typical when tokens live in a credential store the UF cannot read):

```ini
# /opt/splunk/etc/apps/proxmox-monitoring/local/inputs.conf
# Heavy Forwarder polling cluster API (or per-node UF if scoping to one node)

[script:///opt/splunk/etc/apps/proxmox-monitoring/bin/poll_qmigrate_tasks.sh]
disabled = 0
interval = 180
sourcetype = proxmox:task:qmigrate
index = virtualization
source = proxmox:rest_api:tasks:qmigrate

[monitor:///var/log/pveproxy/access.log]
disabled = 0
sourcetype = proxmox:pveproxy:access
index = virtualization
crcSalt = <SOURCE>
```

Wrapper `poll_qmigrate_tasks.sh`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
PVE_API_TOKEN=$(/opt/splunk/bin/splunk show passwd -auth admin:changeme 2>/dev/null | grep proxmox_api_token | awk '{print $NF}')
PVE_API_HOST="pve-cluster.internal:8006"
export PVE_API_TOKEN PVE_API_HOST
exec /usr/bin/python3 /opt/splunk/etc/apps/proxmox-monitoring/bin/poll_qmigrate_tasks.py
```

The companion `poll_qmigrate_tasks.py` should: load token id and secret (prefer a file with mode 0600, not a world-readable env var in production), request tasks with the filters above, iterate `data`, print `json.dumps(row, separators=(',',':'))` per row, and exit non-zero on HTTP errors so ExecProcessor backoffs are visible. Do not invent a `task_type` field; rely on `type` from PVE. Document TLS: either trust your internal CA in `splunkd` or pin `PVE_API_HOST` to a name that matches the cert.

`inputs.conf` optional stanza for task index directory (if you add later):

```ini
# Example only; requires props for multiline task logs, not the primary JSON line contract
# [monitor:///var/log/pve/tasks/...]
# disabled = 1
# sourcetype = proxmox:task:log
```

Token example (operational, not copy-paste secrets): `pveum user add splunk@pve`, `pveum aclmod` with `PVEVMUser` or a custom role that includes migration read rights, `pveum user token add splunk@pve splunk-poll` with the header form `PVEAPIToken user@realm!tokenid=secret`. Rotate tokens on the same schedule as other break-glass credentials.


## Step 2 Parse and Normalize

`props.conf` below keeps one JSON line per event, binds `_time` to the API `starttime` in epoch seconds, and states that multiline task files are a different sourcetype if you add them. The reason we `coalesce(source_node, node, host)` is that the REST row names the source as `node`, some forwarder transforms rename it, and a minority of ad hoc scripts preface `source_node` before forwarding. The guest id is `coalesce(vmid, id)` because the task object stores vmid in `id` for several Proxmox versions.

`props.conf` for the JSON line stream:

```ini
[proxmox:task:qmigrate]
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
KV_MODE = none
TIME_PREFIX = "starttime"\s*:\s*
TIME_FORMAT = %s
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
category = Structured
description = Proxmox VE QEMU live-migration task records polled from /api2/json/cluster/tasks?typefilter=qmigrate

[proxmox:pveproxy:access]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TIME_PREFIX = ^\S+\s+\S+\s+
TIME_FORMAT = [%d/%b/%Y:%H:%M:%S %z]
EXTRACT-vmid = /qemu/(?<vmid>\d+)/migrate
EXTRACT-target_node = target=(?<target_node>[A-Za-z0-9_\-]+)
description = Proxmox pveproxy HTTP access log; used to track who initiated each migration
```

The `case()` chain on `status_norm` maps the lowercased PVE `status` string to stable buckets: CPU heterogeneity, local disk paths without shared or replicated storage, migration channel loss, auto-converge and downtime pressure, target RAM or disk full, migration TCP/SSH range blocked, QMP parameter failures, ISO attached, SR-IOV or PCI passthrough, corosync or quorum, generic timeouts, and a residual `other_error`. Tuning: after each major PVE upgrade, take ten real failure strings from `/cluster/tasks` and extend the `match()` alternationsŌĆödo not treat the list as closed.

Lookup table for business context (register in `transforms.conf`):

```ini
# /opt/splunk/etc/apps/proxmox-monitoring/lookups/proxmox_vm_inventory.csv
vmid,vm_name,owner_team,sla_class,allowed_target_nodes,pci_passthrough_devices,critical_business_app
100,db-prod-01,db-platform,gold,"pve01,pve02,pve03",,yes
101,oltp-cache-01,app-eng,gold,"pve01,pve02",,yes
102,gpu-ml-01,ml-team,silver,"pve04",nvidia_h100,no
103,jenkins-01,ci-platform,bronze,"pve02,pve03",,no
```

After `stats`, `severity_bucket` encodes: chronic configuration classes (`cpu_flag_mismatch`, `pci_passthrough_present`) as critical, repeated network disconnects, or target resource and cluster membership stress as high, and single-event cases as medium or low. This matches operator intuition: a passthrough GPU guest cannot be live-migrated without architecture change, while a one-off timeout after a long convergence often clears on retry.


## Step 3 Validate

These checks are specific to QEMU online migration task telemetry, not generic Splunk health.

(a) On a shell with `pvesh` or `curl` against the API, run `pvesh get /cluster/tasks --typefilter qmigrate` (flags vary by version) or `GET /api2/json/cluster/tasks?typefilter=qmigrate&limit=20` and confirm the JSON `data` array includes fields this UC expects, especially a recent `UPID:...:qmigrate:...` when someone migrated a test VM. Compare `starttime` and `endtime` to the PVE UI at Datacenter ŌĆö Tasks with the same `upid`.

(b) Known-bad migration (lab): attach a local ISO to a test VM, for example `qm set <vmid> --ide2 local:iso/proxmox-ve_*.iso,media=cdrom`, then `qm migrate <vmid> <target_node> --online` (or the GUI equivalent). PVE should fail quickly with language matching `iso_attached` or ŌĆ£local CD-ROMŌĆØ in the task `status` string. Your next poller window should show a non-OK row with `reason_norm=iso_attached` after the SPL filters.

(c) Splunk: `index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:task:qmigrate" earliest=-1h | head 5` ŌĆö expand the fields: `source_node` or `node`, `vmid` or `id`, `status`, `upid` must be visible if props and the poller are correct.

(d) If you also ingest pveproxy access, run a second search on `sourcetype=proxmox:pveproxy:access` in the same window and join informally on `vmid` to see initiator source addresses versus API task `user`.

(e) Per-task log deep dive: for one failing `upid`, `GET /api2/json/nodes/<node>/tasks/<URL-encoded-UPID>/log` and confirm the `t` text lines line up in time with the `status` string you classŌĆöthis is your escape hatch if `status` is shorter than the log.

(f) Negative control: migrate a small idle VM with shared storage and matching CPU types between two healthy nodes; the row should end `OK` and disappear from the failure-oriented SPL because `status_norm=ok` is filtered out.

(g) Quorum: if you maintain a lab where you can stop corosync on one non-active member safely, a refused migration may surface as `corosync_unhealthy` or membership stringsŌĆötreat that as a validation of the bucket, not a production drill without change approval.


## Step 4 Operationalize

(1) Operator workflow when an alert fires: open the PVE web UI, Datacenter ŌĆö Tasks, paste the `last_upid` from the alert into the filter, and read the full status line. Identify `source_node` and `vmid` from the table. Check free RAM and pressure on the destination with `pvesh get /nodes/<dst>/status` (or the GUI node summary) before re-trying. If `cpu_flag_mismatch` appears, open `/etc/pve/qemu-server/<vmid>.conf` on a cluster editor session and read the `cpu:` line: heterogenous EPYC vs Xeon clusters often need a conservative model such as `kvm64` for movable guests, which is a change request, not a silent toggle. If `local_storage_unreachable` appears, review whether the guest uses local ZFS with `--with-local-disks` and whether `zfs send/recv` or pvesr alignment existsŌĆöcross-check UC-19.1.40 for storage replication, not Ceph health (UC-19.1.39).

(2) Escalation: repeated `cpu_flag_mismatch` across many `vmid`s triggers a platform-eng review of cluster CPU feature policies and golden images. `pci_passthrough_present` or `qmp_command_failed` with SR-IOV in the inventory should route to the application owner: live migration is not a supported path until devices are removed or replaced with paravirt equivalents. `corosync_unhealthy` with concurrent cluster alerts pages the cluster admin before storage or VM teams re-attempt large-scale evacuations. Use Pager throttles: one open incident per `source_node`+`vmid` per 30 minutes unless `severity_bucket=critical`, where immediate bridge is allowed.

(3) Silencing and drilldown: honor maintenance windows with a `maintenance_flag` on nodes from your change system; when set, demote non-critical buckets to a ticket. Keep ISO attach failures unsilenced in production tiers unless the guest is a known ŌĆ£goldenŌĆØ image lab. The drilldown URL pattern is `https://<source_node_fqdn>:8006/?#v1:0:tasks` (name resolution must match the cert). Pass `earliest` from `last_failure` in the tokenized child search. Optional: a dashboard panel that `append`s `sourcetype=proxmox:pveproxy:access` with the same `vmid` in the 15 minutes before `starttime` to show the initiating client.


## Step 5 Continuous Improvement

(1) Tune `severity_bucket` with a clone search that prepends `streamstats` over 7 days by `source_node` and `vmid` to count streaks of `other_error` after maintenanceŌĆöif a guest always fails once then succeeds, widen the `medium` window or add a `dedup` on `last_upid`. Pair with a second scheduled search: `| bin _time span=1h | stats count by source_node, reason_norm` to find chronic patterns during backup hours or batch jobs that dirty memory.

(2) Enrichment: call `/api2/json/cluster/resources?type=node` in a separate poller and `lookup` memory pressure: if a `target_resource_exhausted` failure lands when the target is at 90% RAM by design (dense GPU nodes), route to capacity review rather than an immediate re-page. For SOAR, you might script a one-time `qm set` CPU model after explicit approvalŌĆönever auto-change production `cpu` without a ticket, but a lab can demonstrate an automated `kvm64` retry once.

(3) LXC: `lxcmigrate` (restart-based) is a different `type`ŌĆöeither extend the poller to emit `sourcetype=proxmox:task:lxcmigrate` with parallel SPL, or keep this UC strictly QEMU and document the gap in `exclusions`.

(4) Drift: Proxmox can add post-copy or bandwidth stringsŌĆörevisit the `excessive_dirty_pages` bucket when you enable new QEMU capabilities.

**Primary saved search (must match the `spl` field in this JSON file character-for-character):**

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:task:qmigrate" earliest=-24h@h
| eval source_node = coalesce(source_node, node, host)
| eval vmid = coalesce(vmid, id)
| eval status_norm = lower(coalesce(status, ""))
| where status_norm != "ok" AND status_norm != "running" AND isnotnull(status)
| eval reason_norm = case(
    match(status_norm, "(target host cpu does not support|cpu (model|feature)|incompatible cpu)"), "cpu_flag_mismatch",
    match(status_norm, "(could not migrate.*local|local-disk|local disk image|storage.*not (shared|available))"), "local_storage_unreachable",
    match(status_norm, "(channel disconnected|connection reset|network unreachable|migration network)"), "network_disconnect",
    match(status_norm, "(downtime|migration speed too slow|excessive (dirty|memory)|auto.?converge.*failed)"), "excessive_dirty_pages",
    match(status_norm, "(insufficient (memory|ram)|out of memory|storage.*full|max.*concurrent.*migrations)"), "target_resource_exhausted",
    match(status_norm, "(firewall|port.*blocked|connection refused.*60[0-9]{3})"), "firewall_blocked",
    match(status_norm, "(qmp command.*failed|migrate.set.capabilities|migrate.set.parameters)"), "qmp_command_failed",
    match(status_norm, "(local cd.?rom|iso (image|attached)|cdrom is on local)"), "iso_attached",
    match(status_norm, "(pci.?(e)? passthrough|hostpci|sr.?iov)"), "pci_passthrough_present",
    match(status_norm, "(corosync|quorum|cluster membership)"), "corosync_unhealthy",
    match(status_norm, "got timeout|operation timed out|deadline exceeded"), "timeout",
    1==1, "other_error")
| eval duration_sec = if(isnotnull(endtime) AND isnotnull(starttime), endtime - starttime, null())
| stats latest(_time) as last_failure,
        count as failures_24h,
        values(reason_norm) as reasons,
        values(status) as raw_status_msgs,
        values(target_node) as target_nodes,
        latest(duration_sec) as last_duration_sec,
        latest(upid) as last_upid,
        latest(user) as initiated_by
        by source_node, vmid
| eval severity_bucket = case(
    mvfind(reasons, "cpu_flag_mismatch") >= 0 OR mvfind(reasons, "pci_passthrough_present") >= 0, "critical",
    failures_24h >= 3 AND mvfind(reasons, "network_disconnect") >= 0, "high",
    mvfind(reasons, "target_resource_exhausted") >= 0 OR mvfind(reasons, "corosync_unhealthy") >= 0, "high",
    failures_24h >= 1, "medium",
    1==1, "low")
| sort -failures_24h, +severity_bucket
| table last_failure, source_node, target_nodes, vmid, severity_bucket, failures_24h, reasons, raw_status_msgs, last_duration_sec, initiated_by, last_upid
```

Narrative for reviewers: the `where` clause removes healthy `ok` and in-flight `running` task rows so the alert focuses on failed or anomalous terminal states. The `stats` by `source_node` and `vmid` groups retries in the 24h window, `mvfind` is used to scan multivalue `reasons` for classification, and the final `table` is suitable for a dashboard, email, and on-call handoff with `last_upid` as the key back into PVE. For drilldowns, map `source_node` to a DNS name your browser trusts.


## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:task:qmigrate" earliest=-24h@h
| eval source_node = coalesce(source_node, node, host)
| eval vmid = coalesce(vmid, id)
| eval status_norm = lower(coalesce(status, ""))
| where status_norm != "ok" AND status_norm != "running" AND isnotnull(status)
| eval reason_norm = case(
    match(status_norm, "(target host cpu does not support|cpu (model|feature)|incompatible cpu)"), "cpu_flag_mismatch",
    match(status_norm, "(could not migrate.*local|local-disk|local disk image|storage.*not (shared|available))"), "local_storage_unreachable",
    match(status_norm, "(channel disconnected|connection reset|network unreachable|migration network)"), "network_disconnect",
    match(status_norm, "(downtime|migration speed too slow|excessive (dirty|memory)|auto.?converge.*failed)"), "excessive_dirty_pages",
    match(status_norm, "(insufficient (memory|ram)|out of memory|storage.*full|max.*concurrent.*migrations)"), "target_resource_exhausted",
    match(status_norm, "(firewall|port.*blocked|connection refused.*60[0-9]{3})"), "firewall_blocked",
    match(status_norm, "(qmp command.*failed|migrate.set.capabilities|migrate.set.parameters)"), "qmp_command_failed",
    match(status_norm, "(local cd.?rom|iso (image|attached)|cdrom is on local)"), "iso_attached",
    match(status_norm, "(pci.?(e)? passthrough|hostpci|sr.?iov)"), "pci_passthrough_present",
    match(status_norm, "(corosync|quorum|cluster membership)"), "corosync_unhealthy",
    match(status_norm, "got timeout|operation timed out|deadline exceeded"), "timeout",
    1==1, "other_error")
| eval duration_sec = if(isnotnull(endtime) AND isnotnull(starttime), endtime - starttime, null())
| stats latest(_time) as last_failure,
        count as failures_24h,
        values(reason_norm) as reasons,
        values(status) as raw_status_msgs,
        values(target_node) as target_nodes,
        latest(duration_sec) as last_duration_sec,
        latest(upid) as last_upid,
        latest(user) as initiated_by
        by source_node, vmid
| eval severity_bucket = case(
    mvfind(reasons, "cpu_flag_mismatch") >= 0 OR mvfind(reasons, "pci_passthrough_present") >= 0, "critical",
    failures_24h >= 3 AND mvfind(reasons, "network_disconnect") >= 0, "high",
    mvfind(reasons, "target_resource_exhausted") >= 0 OR mvfind(reasons, "corosync_unhealthy") >= 0, "high",
    failures_24h >= 1, "medium",
    1==1, "low")
| sort -failures_24h, +severity_bucket
| table last_failure, source_node, target_nodes, vmid, severity_bucket, failures_24h, reasons, raw_status_msgs, last_duration_sec, initiated_by, last_upid
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: count of `severity_bucket` in {critical, high} for the time picker. (2) Table: `last_failure`, `source_node`, `target_nodes`, `vmid`, `severity_bucket`, `failures_24h`, `reasons`, `last_duration_sec`, `initiated_by`, `last_upid`, with drilldown to raw events and a child search over `proxmox:pveproxy:access` by `vmid`. (3) Timechart: count of `failures_24h` by `source_node` when preprocessed with `timechart` on `last_failure`. (4) Optional sparkline: `last_duration_sec` for migrations over 30 minutes to catch slow network-bound transfers. (5) Map or topology panel: optional; not required. (6) **Drill** token passes `source_node`+`vmid`+`last_upid` into a search filtered to the failure window with `proxmox:task:qmigrate` and optional append of access logs.
